source_stig.title: The F5 BIG-IP appliance must record time stamps for audit records that can be mapped to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).
source_stig.checktext:
From the BIG-IP GUI:
1. System.
2. Platform.
3. Verify that "UTC" is configured for "Time Zone".

From the BIG-IP Console, issue the following commands:

tmsh list sys ntp timezone

Note: This must return a value of "UTC".

If the BIG-IP appliance is not configured for the UTC time zone, this is a finding.
source_stig.fixtext:
From the BIG-IP GUI:
1. System.
2. Platform.
3. Set "UTC" for "Time Zone".
4. Click "Update".

From the BIG-IP Console, issue the following commands:

tmsh modify sys ntp timezone UTC
tmsh save sys config
source_stig.description:
If time stamps are not consistently applied and there is no common time reference, it is difficult to perform forensic analysis.

Time stamps generated by the application include date and time. Time is commonly expressed in UTC, a modern continuation of Greenwich Mean Time (GMT), or local time with an offset from UTC.

pullback_df.match_rule: observed value satisfies required predicate on the shared measurable

pullback_df.finding: 1 measurement(s) observed on the appliance do not satisfy the STIG predicate; see the truth table for exact values.

human_review_row.requirement: The F5 BIG-IP appliance must record time stamps for audit records that can be mapped to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).
human_review_row.why: NTP must define servers AND 'include authenticate' so time sync is authenticated.
human_review_row.tmsh_remediation_command: tmsh modify sys ntp servers add { <approved_ntp1> <approved_ntp2> }; tmsh save sys config

violations: NTP must define servers AND 'include authenticate' so time sync is authenticated.
